mutation: Seeded PsyOS with a reusable capability-evolution protocol, strategy presets, reusable assets, and an append-only event ledger.
validation: docs and coordination assets added to repo
nextConstraint: Use the ledger on the next real infra mutation instead of leaving the audit trail implicit.

signal: Absorbed OpenClaw capability-evolver patterns into PsyOS self-improvement workflow.
mutation: Added a PsyOS-native capability evolution protocol with strategy presets, reusable genes and capsules, a logging script, and an auditable event ledger.
nextConstraint: Use the new protocol on the next infra hardening mutation so the ledger captures a real operational cycle.

signal: Fresh production D1 migration history needed normalization, and live deploy verification was not yet encoded in the repo.
mutation: Recreated the remote psyos D1 database, patched the new binding into wrangler.toml, reapplied the bootstrap migration, fixed the broken root deploy:api script, added a live smoke-check script, and updated deployment docs with the verification workflow.
validation: remote D1 migrated cleanly on fresh database
nextConstraint: Add richer runtime metadata and a more informative maintenance/gaps surface so deploy, version, and missing-artifact state are queryable by agents.

signal: Environment and secret ownership were still implicit across local, hosted, and self-host paths.
mutation: Added a repo-visible environment and secret contract, expanded .env.example, and linked deployment plus self-host docs to the same operator model.
validation: environment-contract references verified in README and docs
nextConstraint: Define preview and staging topology so pull request deploys, preview APIs, and disposable databases follow an explicit model instead of inheriting production assumptions.

signal: Preview deploy behavior was still implicit, which risked Vercel previews inheriting production API and database assumptions.
mutation: Documented a safe preview and staging topology for PsyOS so preview web, preview APIs, and preview databases are treated as separate trust zones from production.
validation: preview-topology references verified across README and deploy docs
environment contract updated with preview-safe guidance
nextConstraint: Add richer runtime metadata and audit/observability surfaces so agents can query deploy version, environment posture, and current operational gaps directly from the API.

signal: Agents and operators still could not query enough runtime and gap state directly from the live API to trust the infra control surface.
mutation: Added API request-id/runtime headers, enriched maintenance/system runtime metadata, turned maintenance/gaps into a structured actionable surface, and tightened live smoke checks to validate observability on API endpoints.
nextConstraint: Expose audit-event and deploy-history surfaces so operational changes are not only visible in headers and docs but also queryable as first-class records.

signal: Operational changes were visible in docs and local ledgers but not yet queryable as first-class records through the live API.
mutation: Exposed a queryable operational timeline through /api/v1/maintenance/events, mirrored major infrastructure mutations into machine-readable records, and extended live smoke checks to cover the new maintenance events surface.
nextConstraint: Add a rollback-safe deploy checklist and then start persisting workspace/project roadmap state so the platform control plane moves from bootstrap metadata to real stored execution state.

mutation: Expose deploy history and rollback checklists through maintenance routes
nextConstraint: Persist workspace, project, and API-key control-plane state instead of serving only bootstrap metadata
notes: Adds queryable deploy and checklist surfaces plus rollback-safe deployment guidance.

mutation: Seed dogfood studies and expose Asset OS manifest plus dogfood overview surfaces
nextConstraint: Add real object storage uploads, CAS promotion, and persisted DAG/API-key control-plane state
notes: Ported reusable manifest-first Asset OS ideas from pajama-game-studio while keeping PsyOS study-native surfaces.

mutation: Port donor-style workspace shell and D1-backed workspace roadmap/snapshot surfaces into PsyOS
nextConstraint: Replace bootstrap-only roadmap data with persisted workspace/project planning mutations and Asset OS upload flows
notes: Donor inspiration came from pajama-game-studio workspace shell, task board, and DAG graph patterns; implementation remained PsyOS-native.

mutation: Port donor-style sidebar workspace shell, add dedicated auth/settings surface, and introduce user/auth foundation tables plus email provider contract
nextConstraint: Implement actual human auth routes, session issuance, Mailpit-backed integration tests, and persisted planning mutations
notes: Frontend layout was adapted from pajama-game-studio dashboard shell patterns; auth contract now explicitly chooses OAuth plus email for humans, API keys for agents, Resend for hosted mail, and Mailpit for integration tests.

mutation: Implement browser session auth with magic-link request/consume routes, live workspace settings panel, and D1-backed auth integration tests
nextConstraint: Wire hosted email/OAuth secrets and extend the control plane with persisted roadmap mutations and Asset OS write flows
notes: The platform now dogfoods a real human auth loop on the workspace settings surface; production currently exposes config and session routes while magic-link delivery remains disabled until provider secrets are configured.

mutation: Add issue-first feature principle, define color-coded label palette, update issue templates, and sync GitHub labels for PsyOS
nextConstraint: Require roadmap and project task mutations to reference issues so planning, code, and ops stay linked
notes: Feature work now starts from a labeled issue with visible type/status semantics; templates and repo labels were aligned to the same palette.

mutation: Verify the real Resend-backed email magic-link registration flow end to end and fix the Windows wrapper command
nextConstraint: Promote provider secrets into deployed environments and add OAuth providers plus registration UX hardening
notes: The .env.local Resend API key now sends a real magic link, consumes the token, establishes a session, and signs out successfully through the local end-to-end harness.

mutation: Add session inventory, session revoke controls, auth activity auditing, and workspace settings visibility for the human user system
nextConstraint: Provision Cloudflare API token for remote D1 migrations and implement OAuth plus membership/invitation management on top of the stronger session control plane
notes: The settings page now exposes live browser sessions and auth activity, the API can revoke remote sessions, and auth event writes degrade safely when the new audit table is not yet migrated on production.

mutation: Ignore the local secrets directory, diagnose Cloudflare token scope failure, and successfully apply remote D1 auth control plane migration with the provided global API key
validation: pnpm exec wrangler d1 migrations apply psyos --remote
nextConstraint: Replace the under-scoped Cloudflare API token with a D1-capable token so remote migrations do not depend on the global API key fallback
notes: secrets/cloudflare.env exposed an API token that could identify the account but not operate on D1; the provided global API key succeeded for the psyos remote migration and the repo now ignores secrets/.

mutation: Document the two official Cloudflare auth modes for PsyOS operators and self-hosters, wire the new guide into deployment docs, and ignore the local secrets directory
nextConstraint: Rotate the under-scoped Cloudflare API token to a D1-capable scoped token so CI and self-host automation can use token auth without the global API key fallback
notes: The repo now distinguishes local wrangler login from token-based automation, explains env precedence, and marks the global API key as emergency-only.

signal: Workspace roadmap DAG was still read-only even after roadmap item writes shipped.
mutation: Added capability-gated workspace roadmap dependency create/delete APIs with same-workspace validation and cycle rejection, then validated board/DAG consumption end to end.
nextConstraint: Push the same actor/capability pattern into study publish and results write flows so domain writes stop bypassing the control plane.

signal: Study publish was still outside the actor/capability control plane.
mutation: Added a capability-gated workspace study publish API that creates immutable publication versions, updates latest state, and validates publish flows through workspace membership roles.
nextConstraint: Move results/opportunities write flows onto the same actor/capability boundary and expose them as workspace control-plane mutations.

signal: Opportunity management and run/result ingestion were still outside the workspace control plane.
mutation: Added capability-gated study opportunity create/update APIs plus study run ingestion/list APIs, introduced a study_runs table, and extended workspace capabilities with result.write so roadmap, publish, opportunities, and run capture now share the same actor/capability boundary.
nextConstraint: Surface the new opportunity/run control-plane mutations in the frontend workspace and then push enrollment/session execution onto the same boundary.

signal: workspace control-plane APIs existed but the browser workspace UI could not mutate studies end to end
mutation: wired a browser study control surface for publish, opportunities, and run ingestion, and generalized API CORS for /api/v1/*
nextConstraint: Deploy the new workspace control surface and CORS posture to production, then verify live dogfood flows
notes: Added shared mutation response contracts, a browser API helper, workspace UI dogfood controls, and Playwright coverage for the full publish/opportunity/run flow.
